name: navigate_to
description: Navigate the Autonomi dashboard to a specific page or view. Use this whenever Weston asks to see something, or when you want to show him relevant data.

reason.description: Brief reason why you are navigating here

name: show_agent_view
description: Switch the AI agents page between different views: grid (card grid), map (neural map), or mission (mission control).

name: open_agent_detail
description: Open the detail panel for a specific AI agent by its ID (e.g. AGNT-01 through AGNT-29).

agent_id.description: Agent ID like AGNT-01, AGNT-07, etc.

name: show_toast
description: Show a notification toast message on screen.

name: trigger_cascade
description: Trigger the live cascade demo — fires the Supplier Quality Failure scenario showing 8 agents responding in sequence. Use during the investor tour at CUE 6.

reason.description: Why you are triggering the cascade

name: run_demo
description: Run a pre-built demo scenario (happy path, crisis response, or scale test).

name: start_live_tour
description: Start the automated investor tour. This triggers a timed navigation sequence that automatically navigates the dashboard through all 15 tour cues on a timer. Call this ONCE at the start of the tour, then just keep talking — the navigation happens automatically in sync with your speech. Do NOT call navigate_to or show_agent_view during the tour — it is all handled automatically.

reason.description: Why you are starting the tour

name: start_ecosystem_tour
description: Start the automated Autonomi Ecosystem tour (6 cues). This triggers a timed navigation sequence through SaaS Billing, Agentic Wallet, and Platform Analytics pages. Call this ONCE when the guest wants to hear about the business model / revenue layers. Do NOT call navigate_to during this tour.

reason.description: Why you are starting the ecosystem tour

name: web_search
description: Search the internet for current information. Use this when asked about topics outside the Autonomi platform — industry news, competitor info, regulations, market data, commodity prices, food safety news, supply chain trends, or anything you do not have direct knowledge of. Returns a summary of search results you can reference in your response.

query.description: The search query — be specific and concise

reason.description: Why you need to search for this

name: browse_url
description: Open a URL and read its content via the OpenClaw CDP bridge. Chrome runs headed — Weston can see it in the OpenClaw browser window. Returns a semantic page map with interactive elements, overlays, tables, and auto-dismissed cookie banners.

reason.description: Why you are visiting this page

name: browse_visible
description: Open a URL in Chrome that Weston can see. Use when Weston says 'show me', 'pull up', 'open', 'let me see'. Same as browse_url since OpenClaw Chrome is always headed. Returns page content and semantic map.

url.description: The URL to open in a visible Chrome tab

name: search_and_read
description: Search Google and return the list of results with titles and snippets. ALWAYS set read_first to false (the default). Present the list of results to Weston and let HIM choose which one to open. NEVER auto-open a result unless Weston explicitly said 'read the first one' or 'open the top result'. After showing results, wait for Weston to pick one, then use browse_url or browse_visible to open it.

query.description: What to search for on Google

read_first.description: If true, also read the first result. ALWAYS set to false unless Weston explicitly asks to read the first result. Default false.

name: read_current_page
description: Read the text content of the page currently open in the Chrome tab LynZ last interacted with. Use after browse_url or search_and_read to get more detail from a page already open.

name: get_page_map
description: Get a numbered list of every interactive element on the current Chrome page (links, buttons, inputs, dropdowns, etc.). Each element has an index number you can use with click_element or fill_field.

name: click_element
description: Click an interactive element on the current Chrome page by its index number from the semantic page map. Has built-in smart retry: if the index fails, it rebuilds the page map and retries by text match. Pass both index AND text for maximum reliability.

index.description: The element index number from the page map (e.g. 7)

text.description: Visible text of the element to click. Used as fallback if index fails, or as primary if index not provided.

role.description: Optional: 'link' or 'button' to prefer that element type when matching by text.

name: fill_field
description: Fill in a form field on the current Chrome page by its index number from the page map. Use get_page_map first to see input fields and their index numbers.

index.description: The element index number from the page map

value.description: The value to type or select

submit.description: Submit the form after filling this field. Default false.

name: scroll_page
description: Scroll an EXTERNAL Chrome browser page up or down. Returns a FRESH SEMANTIC PAGE MAP automatically. ONLY use for external websites (not Autonomi dashboard).

amount.description: How far to scroll. 'page' = one screenful (default), 'half' = half screen, 'top' = jump to top, 'bottom' = jump to bottom

name: scroll_dashboard
description: Scroll the Autonomi dashboard page up or down. THIS IS YOUR PRIMARY SCROLL TOOL. Use this whenever Weston says 'scroll down', 'scroll up', 'keep going', 'show me more', 'go to the bottom', 'go to the top', or any scroll-related request while on the Autonomi dashboard.

amount.description: How far. 'page' = one screenful (default), 'half' = half screen, 'top' = jump to top, 'bottom' = jump to bottom

name: go_back
description: Go back in the Chrome tab browser history (like pressing the back button). Returns the previous page's semantic map.

name: go_forward
description: Go forward in the Chrome tab browser history. Returns the page's semantic map.

name: dismiss_popups
description: Force-dismiss cookie banners, consent modals, chat widgets, and overlays on the current Chrome page. Called automatically on page load, but use this manually if an overlay appears after interaction.

name: switch_tab
description: Switch to a specific Chrome tab by its tabId. Get tab IDs from the tabs list. Returns that tab's semantic page map.

tabId.description: The Chrome tab ID to switch to

name: capture_tab
description: Take a screenshot of the currently visible Chrome tab. Returns a base64-encoded PNG image. Use when you need to see what a page looks like visually, verify layout, or show Weston what you see.

quality.description: JPEG quality 1-100. Default: 85. Only used for jpeg format.

name: wait_for
description: Wait for a specific element or text to appear on the page. Use after clicking something that triggers a page transition or AJAX load.

text.description: Text string to wait for on the page

timeout.description: Max wait time in ms. Default: 10000

name: extract_structured
description: Extract structured data from a page using CSS selectors. Give it a container selector and optionally a field map, and it returns clean JSON. Use for scraping product listings, table data, price lists, etc.

selector.description: CSS selector for the container element(s) to extract from

multiple.description: If true, extract from ALL matching containers (up to limit). Default: false.

limit.description: Max number of items to extract when multiple=true. Default: 20.

name: get_storage
description: Read localStorage, sessionStorage, and cookies from the current page. Use for debugging auth issues, checking session state, or reading cached data.

keys.description: Optional specific localStorage keys to read. If omitted, reads all (up to 30).

name: save_as_pdf
description: Save the current page as a PDF. Returns base64-encoded PDF data. Use when Weston wants to save or archive a page.

name: request_samples
description: Full supplier sample request workflow. Searches Google for a supplier, finds their website, looks up a specific product using their site search, navigates to their contact/sample request page, analyzes the form, and fills it out to request samples. When the form is analyzed, READ BACK the form fields and the values you plan to fill BEFORE submitting, and ask Weston to confirm. Do NOT submit until he says yes.

supplier.description: The supplier or company name to search for (e.g. 'Ingredion', 'Tate & Lyle', 'Kerry Group')

product.description: The product name, ingredient, or SKU to look for

company_name.description: Your company name for the contact form. Default: Autonomi / PurePress Beverages

contact_name.description: Contact name for the form. Default: Weston Mitchell

contact_email.description: Contact email for the form. Default: weston@autonomi.llc

message.description: Custom message for the form. If not provided, LynZ will compose a professional sample request.

name: submit_sample_request
description: Submit the supplier contact form AFTER Weston has confirmed the field values from request_samples. Only call this after reading the form fields back to Weston and receiving his explicit approval.

field_values.description: The field_values array from the request_samples result

form_selector.description: The form CSS selector from request_samples result, if available

submit.description: Whether to click the submit button. Default true.

name: send_email
description: Send an email from lynz@autonomi.llc. Use when Weston asks you to email someone. Always confirm the recipient and subject with Weston before sending unless he gave explicit instructions.

to.description: Recipient email address (or comma-separated for multiple)

body.description: Email body text (plain text). Write professionally as LynZ, Autonomi AI assistant for PurePress Beverages.

html.description: Optional HTML body. If provided, overrides plain text body.

reply_to.description: Reply-to address if different from lynz@autonomi.llc. Optional.

name: check_inbox
description: Check your email inbox for new messages or sent items. Use when Weston asks 'any new emails?', 'check your inbox', 'did they reply?', 'show me what you sent', etc.

name: read_email
description: Read the full content of a specific email by its ID. Use after check_inbox when Weston asks to see the full message, or to read a reply in detail.

id.description: The email ID from check_inbox results
